title: All-source assessment: Iran-Lebanon-UK threat triad converges in single 71-minute window
contents:
# All-source assessment: Iran-Lebanon-UK threat triad converges in single 71-minute window

## Overview
Multi-source intelligence aggregation across the 21:00-22:11 UTC window on April 30, 2026 captured a convergence of three threat lines: Iranian air-defence engagements over Tehran with explicit IRGC strike threats against US warships, an IDF strike package against Hezbollah following a drone attack that wounded 12 soldiers near Shomera, and a UK national terrorism threat-level rise to 'severe' after a London antisemitic attack. Cross-domain correlation suggests escalation pressure across Gulf, Levant, and Western theatres simultaneously.

## Indicator Triad
- IMINT/SIGINT/HUMINT (Iran): Tehran air-defence batteries firing during evening hours; IRGC official statement amplifies threat against US warships
- HUMINT/OSINT (Levant): IDF release of strike footage on Hezbollah structures in southern Lebanon; 12 IDF wounded near Shomera; combat death of Sgt. Liem Ben-Hamo
- HUMINT (UK): JTAC raises threat level to 'severe' after London antisemitic incident

## Pattern Analysis
All three indicators broke or refreshed inside the same 71-minute window. The clustering pattern resembles operational-preparation tempo seen earlier in the conflict immediately ahead of significant kinetic phases. Confidence assessment: medium-high based on multi-source corroboration; primary risk vector remains potential IRGC action against US naval assets in the Persian Gulf.

## Tags
all-source-intelligence, iran, lebanon, uk, threat-convergence, escalation, multi-domain
summary: All-source intelligence captured convergence of Iranian air-defence engagements over Tehran, IDF strikes on Hezbollah in southern Lebanon, and the UK raising its terror threat level to 'severe' all within the 21:00-22:11 UTC window on April 30, 2026.
